rules.title: Environments are declared and agree across planes
rules.lesson: the descriptor’s tier list, the forge’s environments, and the workflows’ environment: keys are three declarations of one fact; drift is how "we have a staging" becomes untrue. Set agreement is deterministic
rules.rationale: The descriptor’s tier list, the forge’s environments, and the workflows’ environment: keys are three declarations of one fact; drift between them is how "we have a staging" becomes untrue. Set agreement is deterministic; forge unreachable degrades to descriptor==workflow, labeled.
rules.fix: Reconcile the three surfaces: declare each real environment in the descriptor, create missing forge environments, remove phantom ones.

rules.title: Every declared tier has an automated deploy path
rules.lesson: a declared environment nothing deploys to is a diagram, not a tier. A tier deployed by an external system the checker can’t see is a documented deviation
rules.rationale: A declared environment nothing deploys to is a diagram, not a tier. Per DORA, any version deployable to any environment on demand is the bar; a tier deployed by an external system the checker can’t see is a documented deviation. Heuristic line-scan (no YAML parser).
rules.fix: Add a deploy job for the orphaned tier; externally-deployed tier → pipeline jdg new --kind deviation --subject ENV-02 --reason "tier <name> deployed by <system>" --review-by <date>.

rules.title: The dev tier deploys on merge to the integration branch
rules.lesson: pre-prod tiers receive frequent automated deploys from the integration branch; research-narrowed to "on merge / frequent", never a daily quota. Stays team warning
rules.rationale: Pre-production tiers receive frequent automated deploys from the integration branch, and each passed tier gates the next. Research-narrowed: no primary source prescribes a literal daily deploy to a dev environment, so this checks "on merge / frequent", never a daily quota. Heuristic line-scan.
rules.fix: Point the dev-deploy workflow’s on: push: branches: at the integration branch.

rules.title: Production promotion is gated
rules.lesson: for high-criticality systems, promotion into production requires explicit human approval — the forges ship exactly this mechanism, NIST CM-3 supplies the compliance-grade why. Deterministic forge read → blocker-at-critical is lawful
rules.rationale: For high-criticality systems, promotion into production requires explicit human approval — the forges ship exactly this mechanism and NIST supplies the compliance-grade why. Deterministic forge read, so blocker-at-critical is lawful; offline is a labeled SKIP, never a silent PASS.
rules.fix: Add required reviewers to the production environment (GitHub: environment → deployment protection rules → required reviewers; GitLab: protected environments + deployment approvals).

rules.title: Staging data realism is reviewed
rules.lesson: pre-prod validation is only meaningful if staging resembles production, including data; data realism is inherently uncheckable at rest, so the whole rule is a dated human judgment. Strongest quote is data-pipeline-scoped; prompt states its context
rules.rationale: Pre-prod validation is only meaningful if staging resembles production, including data; data realism is inherently uncheckable at rest, so the entire rule is a dated human judgment. The sign-off prompt asks: staging data volume/shape reviewed against production within the review period; gaps named.
rules.fix: Review staging data realism with the team and record: pipeline jdg new --kind sign-off --subject ENV-05 --reason "staging data is <full copy|representative subset|synthetic> of prod; reviewed <date>" --review-by <date>.

rules.title: A pre-prod validation stage runs before prod promotion
rules.lesson: something automated must exercise staging before prod gets the change — smoke at minimum. Presence is tree-checkable; adequacy is the sign-off fallback. Stays warning at critical (R2)
rules.rationale: Something automated must exercise staging before prod gets the change — smoke at minimum, load where traffic demands it. Presence is tree-checkable; adequacy for the failure-cost tier is the sign-off fallback. Stays a warning at critical (R2).
rules.fix: Add a validation job to the staging workflow (or between staging and prod jobs); external validation rig → dated sign-off naming it.
